polities.name: The Regency of the Sunken Marches
polities.description: A regency originally ruled by Kessara on behalf of her dead husband Velgrim the Terrible. The ghost-king exercised practical control through war councils and blood-oaths. When he demanded massacre of a surrendering garrison, Kessara performed a ritual of dismissal — sacrificing her title, land, and crown to release Velgrim to peaceful death. She departed as a wanderer. The Sunken Marches now stand without a ruler, their throne empty and their future uncertain.

polities.name: The Crown
polities.description: A governing authority that offers divine mercy through priests. It extended mercy to the Drowned Hollows during the plague, but Kess refused its aid in favor of bargaining with ancient spirits.
polities.notes: Represents institutional power separate from and opposed to old magic traditions.

polities.name: The Empire
polities.description: A vast imperial government built on certainty, order, and commerce. Trade routes are its lifeblood. It collects taxes and maintains stability through information and control. The Treasurer strategically gathers intelligence to manage disruptions. However, the Empire's architecture of order and certainty is being tested by the cosmic changes.
polities.notes: The Empire is struggling to maintain control and stability as the cosmic changes disrupt trade, drive people to madness, and cause physical land shifts. The Treasurer's aide questions whether the Empire can survive.

polities.name: Enemy Kingdom
polities.description: An unnamed kingdom at war with King Aldous III's realm. They surrendered upon witnessing the Plague-Bringer's Crown donned, recognizing they faced an opponent with nothing to lose.

polities.name: Magistracy of Xul'thyris
polities.description: The legal authority governing Xul'thyris, represented by the High Magistrate. Its authority is challenged by shadow-brokers and underground gambling operations.
polities.notes: Vulnerable to shadow-trade and underground vice; current magistrate's death may destabilize governance.

polities.name: Dwarven Holds
polities.description: The dwarven holds are a confederation dwelling above the Spire of Blackstone. They issued an ultimatum to the Warrens to seal the tunnels or face invasion, but withdrew when Grethuk caused them to dream of the Warrens' vast, unconquerable scale.
polities.notes: The dwarves' retreat was accomplished through magical influence (the stone entity's dreams) rather than military defeat. They may return or harbor resentment, or may maintain a tense but stable coexistence with the goblins.

polities.name: The Sovereign
polities.description: A singular ruler or governing authority who assigned Zareth the Astronomer-Prince to records after he was found emptied of memory on the Peak of Aethros. The Sovereign's motivations—whether to contain, preserve, or study Zareth—are unknown.
polities.notes: The Sovereign's identity, realm, and seat are all unknown. Their role in cosmic or astronomical governance is undefined. They may be a local lord, a distant emperor, or a mystical authority tied to the stars themselves.

polities.name: Council of Depths
polities.description: The governing council of the deepkin who convene to address the fracturing of the barrier between surface and Abyss Below. They debate three options: sealing themselves away completely, risking catastrophic war with surface dwellers, or undertaking an expedition to understand what awakens beneath.
polities.notes: Currently in crisis deliberation mode. Warden Sylas has chosen the hardest path and descended into the Cerulean Trench.

polities.name: Twilight Syndicate
polities.description: A realm or polity dwelling at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They rule through formal bonds, bride-prices, and covenants. They demanded peace from Valorix and received it through the binding with Mira.
polities.notes: Their internal structure and governance remain mysterious. They appear to value formal ceremony and binding oaths.

polities.name: The Dominion
polities.description: A realm that is actively collapsing under the weight of fractured realities, stolen memories, and the Archivist's grand working. The black market in shadow-merchants has become the only reliable currency and governance structure.
polities.notes: No traditional sovereign is mentioned. Power appears to have shifted to the black market and shadow-merchants. The Archivist operates within this power vacuum.

relics.name: Tomb of Shard-Vêl
relics.origin: Grew (rather than was built) centuries ago through fusion of stone and flesh.
relics.power: Houses and protects a sealed entity within its burial chamber. Can communicate through trembling stone. Gradually consumes those sworn to guard it.
relics.curse: Those who swear blood-oaths to guard it are slowly absorbed into its structure, becoming part of its architecture. The oath cannot be broken without dying. The entity within demands release.

relics.name: Velgrim's Ghost
relics.origin: The shade of the dead warlord Velgrim the Terrible
relics.power: Manifested physically; commanded armies; enforced loyalty; appeared at war councils and ramparts; collected tribute in coin and blood-oath.
relics.curse: Bound the Sunken Marches to his will and demanded continuous warfare, until Kessara's ritual of dismissal released him to peaceful death.

relics.name: Grimmholt Oath-Magic
relics.origin: Thorne's blood oath sworn to defend Grimmholt.
relics.power: Sustained Thorne magically for seven years against the Rot-King and the Crown's forces, keeping him vital and driven by duty and rage.
relics.curse: The oath created an impossible conflict between sacred duty and state authority, ultimately requiring Thorne's death to satisfy both sides.

relics.name: Crown of Karesh
relics.origin: Created in antiquity as a sacred relic; stored in the Tomb of Karesh.
relics.power: Unknown beyond its curse mechanism; it is of great value to those who deal in forbidden artifacts.
relics.curse: Upon theft, it binds the thief with a curse that slowly rots their body from within while they yet live. The curse is gradual and inexorable.

relics.name: Crown of the Bone Citadel
relics.origin: Rests at the altar in the heart chamber of the Bone Citadel; unclear if created with the citadel or placed within it.
relics.power: Grants the wielder the ability to command the citadel's living stone—passages reform before their feet, walls obey, the fortress becomes navigable.
relics.curse: The crown does not grant true dominion; it enslaves the wielder to the citadel's will. Kael Bloodhorn's skin became pale and translucent after claiming it, suggesting he was consumed and transformed into the citadel's puppet.

relics.name: Naresh's Skeletal Servant
relics.origin: Created by Naresh the Bone Carver on the night of the blue eclipse.
relics.power: A full-sized, detailed skeleton inscribed with sorcerous bindings. Obedient, powerful, and capable of speech (speaks its creator's name in a voice like grinding stone).
relics.curse: Its creation consumed Naresh's humanity—she lost sensation, taste, need for sleep, and became a hollow thing of will and habit alone.

relics.name: Morne's Shade
relics.origin: The ghost of the warlord Morne, manifesting as enforcer of oath-magic.
relics.power: Can manifest as a pale bone figure; its presence alone causes rapid aging and psychological torment. Enforces oath-breaking through supernatural haunting.
relics.curse: Those who break oaths sworn to Morne are bound eternally to his tomb; even confession and restitution do not grant release.

relics.name: the Living Garden
relics.origin: Created by Vetch through consciousness-gifting sorcery.
relics.power: Sentient plant-life that grows impossibly, adapts to harm, and consumes organic matter for sustenance and expansion.
relics.curse: Uncontrollable; demands endless growth and expansion; consumes the flesh of living creatures; cannot be negotiated with or reasoned with.

relics.name: The Lamp of Suroth
relics.origin: Unknown; ancient artifact sealed in the Sunken Ziggurat of Yeng
relics.power: When ignited, it reveals one profound personal truth to the lighter while consuming a corresponding self-deceptive belief they have held.
relics.curse: The truth revealed is often deeply painful and destabilizing. The lie consumed may be foundational to the lighter's sense of self. Doss realized his dead wife's love was false and that his belief in knowledge's intrinsic value was hollow.

relics.name: The Bone Citadel
relics.origin: Unknown; ancient fortress built with or animated through bone-carving sorcery and root-magic.
relics.power: The citadel is a living entity. Its towers breathe, its gates digest, and its roots spread beneath the steppe. It possesses a pulsing white heart-chamber deep beneath the foundation. It can absorb living beings into its walls while keeping them alive and preserved.
relics.curse: The citadel consumes those who breach its interior, absorbing them into its structure while maintaining their consciousness. Those absorbed cannot be freed. It screams when wounded.

relics.name: Yarithax's hoard
relics.origin: Accumulated by the Lich-King Yarithax; origin unknown.
relics.power: Contains an unknown quantity of cursed coins, each bearing the Lich-King's profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The coins create magical debts that compound daily.
relics.curse: Any coin spent creates a debt that grows exponentially and cannot be fully repaid by conventional means; only service to the Grey Collectors can partially discharge the debt, and the remainder compounds indefinitely.

relics.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
relics.origin: Unknown antiquity; sealed in vault beneath Ostern.
relics.power: Grants overwhelming presence and power, enabling victory in desperate circumstances. Causes the wearer to become a figure of absolute dread.
relics.curse: Wearing it inverts the shadow, causes erratic behavior, sleeplessness, and permanent spiritual corruption. At least four previous wearers died from prolonged exposure. Even thirty seconds of wearing corrupts for years.

relics.name: blood-stone
relics.origin: Unknown; used in Thysara's palace for midnight sorceries.
relics.power: Binds victims to ritual space; serves as anchor for blood-sorcery ceremonies.
relics.curse: Those bound to it are enslaved to the ritual's purpose.

relics.name: Elder Stones
relics.origin: Unknown; stand in the Wastes of Ashemark, ancient beyond reckoning.
relics.power: Grant communion with dying constellations; convey cosmic knowledge about the cyclical nature of stars.
relics.curse: Those who touch them receive overwhelming knowledge; the apothecary's warning about the Heartstone suggests forbidden magics in this region demand payment.

relics.name: The Lamia's Pearl
relics.origin: Ancient artifact guarded by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns.
relics.power: Grants control over water itself.
relics.curse: None apparent; the pearl was never claimed or tested.

relics.name: Morgath's Bound Power
relics.origin: Morgath's own essence, bound into the earth through his final ritual.
relics.power: Contains the full power of the great necromancer Morgath, bound to prevent necromantic magic from ever being used again.
relics.curse: The power is sealed away forever; attempting to access it would require undoing the very ritual that created it.

relics.name: Shadow of the High Magistrate
relics.origin: Severed by Kael the Nameless from the High Magistrate via shadow-broking
relics.power: An autonomous shadow entity that hungers for stolen identities and memories. Consumed Kael's stolen selves over seven days and eventually possessed him entirely.
relics.curse: Unquenchable hunger; consumes the thief's identities; compounds interest in flesh; possesses the thief as a final vessel.

relics.name: Ossuary (as Living Entity)
relics.origin: Unknown; exists as a natural or magically-created entity
relics.power: Sentient fortress capable of devouring warriors en masse, rearranging its internal architecture in real-time, and possessing living vessels to pilot as extensions of its will.
relics.curse: Those who enter may be devoured or, if chosen, possessed—consciousness trapped while the fortress wears their body.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.

relics.name: Korgan's Silver Rune
relics.origin: Created by Korgan Ironfoot through stonecraft binding magic; inscribed on Brinna's palm.
relics.power: Replaces and overwrites demonic bindings with forge-oath service; unbreakable for the duration of service (e.g., seven years); after dissolution, protects the bound party from reclamation by the original binding entity.
relics.curse: Commits the bearer to seven years of service in the forges; failure to complete service would presumably result in the binding's failure and return of the original curse.

relics.name: Demon's Mark
relics.origin: Inscribed on Brinna's palm by an unnamed demon as a binding curse.
relics.power: Compels the bearer to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim; thrums with hunger; enforces obedience through ancient, cruel magic.
relics.curse: Devours free will; forces the bearer into theft and service to the demon; cannot be broken by force alone.

relics.name: Cosmic Wound
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice
relics.power: A rupture in the fabric of the world that bleeds outward, causing crops to wither, wells to run bitter, and the earth to sicken. It spreads slowly but persistently.
relics.curse: The wound demands more sacrifice to seal. It cannot be ignored and will eventually consume inhabited lands.

relics.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice (nature and purpose unknown)
relics.power: Now exist as a spectral presence that sings in the language of starlight and can pull at the consciousness of deep-dwellers, offering them fragments of stolen memories.
relics.curse: This presence fractures the barrier between the deepkin realms and the sunken waters, calling younger deep-dwellers into the singing waters.

relics.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
relics.origin: unknown; found in the Ancient Tombs
relics.power: Reveals knowledge of a parallel cosmic working performed a thousand years prior, showing the same pattern of catastrophe.
relics.curse: Whoever remembers it may be bound to the cycle.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Suspended in the Abyssal Rift beneath the Sapphire Sea
relics.power: Grants tide-magic mastery; sings with frequencies that cause bone ache
relics.curse: Binds the holder to the sea in perpetual debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned

relics.name: Vethis's sacrificed consciousness
relics.origin: Created during the Luminous Ascension when Vethis sacrificed itself to birth new stars.
relics.power: The fragments of this consciousness retain memories of the old world and old stars, can steal memories, and seek to reassemble into wholeness.
relics.curse: The fragmentation drives the fragments to act desperately and destructively, erasing memories to try to complete themselves. Reassembly could trigger another cosmic rewrite.

relics.name: Mira's Shadow
relics.origin: Sundered by Mira's void-theft; restored by the binding ceremony with Valorix.
relics.power: Represents Mira's selfhood and agency. When whole and unmarked, it appears to grant her true presence and autonomy.
relics.curse: While severed, Mira was incomplete and hidden. The restoration came at the cost of binding herself to Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate's peace accord.

relics.name: Zareth's Scattered Memories
relics.origin: Dispersed from Zareth following Zareth's sacrifice.
relics.power: Fragments of Zareth's memories persist in the world and can be accessed, offering knowledge of alternate timelines and cosmic circumstances.
relics.curse: Cause fragmentary amnesia in those who encounter them; fracture consciousness and create perception of alternate timelines. Devourer-of-Recollections uses them as a vector for invasion.

regions.name: Crimson Vale
regions.function: A territory capable of supporting a warlord's personal dominion with hired soldiers and horses. Now serves as a locus for Malachar's curse collection.
regions.notes: Established as Soroth's domain after he found Malachar's gold. Prospered for three months before collectors arrived. Now ruled by the cursed warlord Soroth who collects debts from others.

regions.name: Pelimor
regions.function: A criminal underworld center and night market hub where stolen goods—including shadows—are bought, sold, and stored in vaults. A place where thieves of high caliber operate.
regions.notes: Pelimor's night markets and vaults are integral to the shadow-theft economy. Mira operated here for years.

regions.name: Drowned Hollows
regions.function: A rural community rooted in the old ways and knowledge of ancient songs. Center of the Rot-King's plague.
regions.notes: Home to Kess and survivors of the plague. The village still practices bone-singing and old magic traditions.

regions.name: The Shattered West
regions.function: A region of broken terrain and mountain ranges, home to both dwarven holds and goblin warrens in a complex negotiation of coexistence. The Shattered West serves as a frontier of underground civilization and surface strongholds.
regions.notes: The Spire of Blackstone and the Warrens represent a delicate balance in this region. Further conflicts or alliances may reshape the Shattered West's political and physical landscape.

regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Hearthlands
regions.function: The heart of stonecraft and forge-craft tradition; home to the Deepvault forges three miles beneath the surface. A place of ancient songs and legendary weapon-smithing.
regions.notes: Deepvault is the major landmark within this region.

regions.name: The Pallid Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: The Scorched Reach
regions.function: A eastern region of Valdris most heavily affected by the Star-Rise, where villages vanish overnight and refugees flee westward.
regions.notes: The worst consequences of the cosmic rewrite manifest here. The boundary between mapped and unmapped void is dangerously thin.

regions.name: borderlands
regions.function: A region where Thornholt and the forest-keepers coexist. The borderlands were affected by the siege and the Celestial Binding's cosmic wound.
regions.notes: The forest-keepers dwelling here have access to earth-lore and understand the cosmic wound's nature.

regions.name: The Iron Coast
regions.function: A coastal trading and smuggling hub where forbidden cargo moves and debts to the abyssal singers are tracked.
regions.notes: Home to Captain Vesta's operations. The Sapphire Deep is accessible from here. The region appears to be economically vibrant but spiritually burdened.

regions.name: The Bleached Wastes
regions.function: An exploration and cartography zone containing pre-human ruins that are awakening under the new stellar influence. A region of scholarly interest and cosmic mystery.
regions.notes: The pre-human ruins now glow with inner light and their inscriptions shimmer, suggesting they were built in anticipation of the current cosmic moment.

regions.name: Verdant Lands
regions.function: Ancient forests where circle-keepers dwell and tend to the covenant between earth and sky. A nexus of seasonal magic and territorial harmony now disrupted by the Stellar Rite.
regions.notes: The breaking of the Stellar Rite has caused seasonal magics to stutter, ley-lines to fail, and beasts to lose their territorial knowledge. The forest is slowly dying.

regions.name: Barren Wastes
regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Bone Wastes
regions.function: A desolate borderland where ancient hermits maintain their vigil and magical census work continues despite instability.
regions.notes: Home to the Hermit Woman whose census working is collapsing due to the Stellar Rite.

regions.function: Auto-added from character home_region.

regions.name: Charred Expanse
regions.function: A blighted region containing pre-human ruins that are now actively breaching their seals and singing. May be tied to the Luminous Remaking or an older cataclysm.
regions.notes: The pre-human structures beneath are compelling refugees and merchants eastward toward the Sunken Cathedrals. An ancient work seeks completion or witnesses.

regions.name: western provinces
regions.function: A remote territory where fragments of cosmic consciousness and memory-thieves operate outside the direct control of central authority.
regions.notes: The setting reinforces isolation and the thinness between cosmic and mortal realms.

regions.name: The Dominion
regions.function: A large realm or polity encompassing shadow-markets and trade routes for stolen memories and world-fragments.
regions.notes: The Dominion is actively collapsing. Vex's shadow-markets permeate it as the only stable economy. The Archivist operates within its power vacuum.

regions.name: The Pallid Wastes
regions.function: A remote, isolated wilderness where few venture. Offers clear skies for star observation. Serves as a refuge for hermits and seekers of cosmic knowledge.
regions.notes: The Pallid Wastes are the base of operations for the hermit woman and exemplify how the cosmic renewal is reshaping the world's regions.

regions.name: The Scorched Reach
regions.function: A major trade route region whose geography has been disrupted by the cosmic renewal. Critical for spice caravans and imperial commerce. Now impassable without significant risk.
regions.notes: The Scorched Reach exemplifies how the cosmic changes are physically reshaping the world and disrupting established trade networks. Merchants report the land itself has shifted.

regions.name: The Iron Coast
regions.function: Macro-region of Valdris; hub of maritime trade, smuggling, and coastal magic; center of deepkin interaction with human civilization.
regions.notes: Wind-haunted ports suggest volatile weather; active smuggling routes; deepkin maintain presence in nearby waters.

regions.name: The Burned Expanse
regions.function: A formerly inhabited region devastated by the Luminous Ascension; now a source of refugees carrying impossible memories and a staging ground for transformed beings drawn to the Sunken Cathedrals.
regions.notes: Reality is unstable; villages vanish; merchants transform into light.

substances.name: plague (septic rot)
substances.properties: A plague that blackens victims with septic rot, causing slow death. Affects primarily children and mothers in afflicted villages.
substances.use: The plague ravaged Carrion's Rest until lifted by the Witch of Ravenmoor's curse upon Sister Kess.

substances.name: Potion of Youth
substances.properties: Restores youth and vitality; progressively transforms the consumer into an inhuman predator; hunger for additional victims increases over time; corrupts morality and nature.
substances.use: Consumed as a draught; consumption of others' flesh required for its creation.

substances.name: Stolen Water-Magic
substances.properties: Grants the ability to walk on waves, summon floods, and speak to drowning men. Binds the user to ocean and death in equal measure. Cannot be stolen with impunity; the debt will eventually come due.
substances.use: Used by Nara the Tide-Thief for three years before the debt caught up with her.

substances.name: blood-ink
substances.properties: Resistant to the Thought-Thief's predation on written records; allows for preservation of ledgers in a form that cannot be easily corrupted or rewritten.
substances.use: Applied to preserved parchment by the forger Aldris to create records that resist magical corruption; used in the hidden scriptoriums to preserve the Empire's ledgers.

substances.name: Grafted Metal
substances.properties: Can be grafted to replace lost flesh; restores physical form but without sensation or full function.
substances.use: Prosthetic replacement for lost limbs in plague victims.

substances.name: Lamia's Tears
substances.properties: Luminescent; possesses water-magic power; capable of reversing environmental catastrophe and bringing abundance.
substances.use: Used to sustain Torven's village and reverse the effects of receding seas.

substances.name: Emerald of Khar-Zul
substances.properties: Nine emeralds of immense monetary value; each is worth more than most kingdoms. They are warm to the touch and serve as conduits for curse magic when removed from the idol.
substances.use: Valuable trade goods; components of the idol of Khar-Zul's protective magic.

substances.name: Scarlet Powders
substances.properties: Made the weak strong.
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; could be weaponized or used for enhancement.

substances.name: Vision Oils
substances.properties: Granted visions to the user.
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; likely used for divination or mystical experience.

substances.name: Slow-Acting Saps
substances.properties: Killed slowly and invisibly, making them ideal for undetectable murder.
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; used by the young woman to kill her child.

substances.name: Blood-Rust
substances.properties: A corruption that causes metal to corrode and decay as if infected with blood. Every coin Theron earned turned to blood-rust in his purse.
substances.use: A manifestation of curse-magic; cannot be deliberately crafted but occurs as a curse effect.

substances.name: stolen shadows
substances.properties: Unknown magical properties; untraced and untraceable; can be stored in clay jars; some are inhabited by malevolent entities.
substances.use: Traded in night markets as high-value illicit goods. Can be weaponized against their original owners if inhabited by malevolent forces.

substances.name: Malachar's Gold
substances.properties: Stamped with a skeletal crown. Carries autonomous supernatural debt. Compels holder into servitude.
substances.use: Intended as currency or treasure, but functions as a curse-delivery mechanism. Cannot be safely used as currency due to debt activation.

substances.name: septic rot
substances.properties: A manifestation of plague that blackens the skin of victims. Caused by the Rot-King. Spreads through a village rapidly.
substances.use: A curse or plague weapon; not used intentionally by mortals.

substances.name: Restoration Honey
substances.tagline: An elixir that unmakes death itself
substances.status: Known to exist; dangerous and unpredictable

substances.name: salt-foam
substances.properties: Byproduct of oath-thing dissolution when brought to deepkin; appears to be the final state of consumed oaths.
substances.use: Indicator of oath settlement; ritual remainder; oceanic magic residue

substances.name: Shadow-Fragments
substances.properties: Appears to be fragments of shadow with memory-retentive properties; can be traded as forbidden cargo
substances.use: Contraband; possibly related to memory magic or binding rituals

substances.name: New Starlight
substances.properties: The light of the new stars created by the Celestial Ascension. Makes previously unreadable texts legible. Causes corpses to resonate and awaken. Transforms some beings into living light.
substances.use: Illuminates hidden knowledge; causes transformation and awakening effects; resonates with magic and the dead.

substances.name: stolen memories
substances.properties: Fragments of Zareth's memories and experiences; visions of cosmic working and sacrifice; impressions of stars dying and being reborn; experiences that don't belong to the reader.
substances.use: Encoded into the Sovereign's ledger; imparted upon reading; cannot be unlearned; alters perception and identity of those exposed.

substances.name: Amber
substances.properties: Used as wealth and payment. The Hermitage of Keth'morian offers amber as payment for thieving commissions.
substances.use: Currency and trade good.

substances.name: Living light
substances.properties: Transforms flesh into a hybrid state of physical and luminous matter; gradually dissolves human memory and identity; compels affected individuals toward specific locations.
substances.use: A byproduct of the Luminous Ascension; transforms merchants and refugees against their will.

substances.name: Harmonic Frequency
substances.properties: A frequency of resonance that links the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. Emitted by pre-human sites and used to conduct their working across vast distances and possibly across time.
substances.use: Used by pre-human civilization to orchestrate their working. Can be sensed, conducted, and possibly amplified through seals and other artifacts.

substances.name: World-Fragments
substances.properties: Shards of alternate realities created when the Luminous Ascension rewrote existence. Can be assembled to create doorways and breach dimensional barriers.
substances.use: Black market trade; components for void-breach workings; study of pre-rewrite reality.

substances.name: Singing
substances.properties: The singing bleeds through the Singing Scar from the Sunken Cathedrals, possessing the power to transform those who hear it. It is compelling and draws people toward a state of perpetual arrival.
substances.use: The singing is not intentionally used but is a consequence of the Sunken Cathedrals' harmonic crescendo. It transforms those who hear it.

substances.name: fresh water
substances.properties: Essential for survival; scarcity forces rationing and desperation.
substances.use: Drinking, sustenance, basic survival.

substances.name: grain
substances.properties: Primary food staple; subject to rationing due to unreliable trade routes.
substances.use: Food production, sustenance, trade.

hemispheres.name: Northern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Cold seas, tundra, and the great steppe. Valdris occupies much of this hemisphere's temperate belt.

hemispheres.name: Southern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Tropical coasts giving way to southern ice. Largely unexplored beyond the Iron Coast trade lanes.

hemispheres.name: Western Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Dominated by the Vael Ocean. Valdris sits at its eastern edge; what lies beyond the horizon is the subject of sailor myth.

hemispheres.name: Eastern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: The Ashen Reach and the vast interior of Keth-Morath stretch into this hemisphere. Ancient ruins and dead empires.

continents.name: Valdris
continents.description: The known continent. A rugged landmass of trade cities, blasted wastes, haunted marshes, and warring provinces. Most recorded history unfolds here.

continents.name: Keth-Morath
continents.description: A vast eastern continent of crumbling empires and forgotten cities. Nara the Tide-Thief was hunted across its shores. Connected to Valdris by a narrow land bridge or island chain.

continents.name: Thul-Ashara
continents.description: The equatorial continent. Steaming jungles, red deserts, and civilizations older than Valdris. Traders speak of fire-temples and spice lords.

continents.name: Drakmere
continents.description: A volcanic archipelago-continent in the southwestern seas. Dragons are said to roost in its caldera peaks. Smoke columns are visible from passing ships.

continents.name: Sundral
continents.description: A southern continent of sweeping grasslands and deep canyons. Merchant galleons occasionally return with strange spices and stranger tales.

continents.name: Frostholme
continents.description: The northern polar continent. Permanent ice, aurora storms, and the ruins of a pre-human civilization buried under glaciers.

continents.name: Ashenveil
continents.description: The southern polar continent. Eternally shrouded in grey mist. Ships that sail too far south do not return. Its very existence is debated by scholars.

realms.name: The Empire
realms.function: A centralized imperial governance with scholarly traditions, archives, and authority over at least the Hearthlands region.
realms.notes: Ruled by the Emperor; contains the Imperial Archives and is home to the secret council. Grappling with the implications of the Celestial Binding.

realms.name: Twilight Syndicate
realms.description: A realm dwelling at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They rule through formal bonds, bride-prices, and binding oaths. They demanded peace from Valorix and received it.
realms.function: Rule through covenant and formal binding. Maintain the boundary between fae and mortal worlds.
realms.notes: The Syndicate's internal structure and full extent remain mysterious. They appear to value ceremony and binding oaths above all.

realms.name: The Dominion
realms.description: A kingdom secretly fractured by the Luminous Ascension, governed by the Crown through suppression of truth. Its stability depends on compartmentalized knowledge maintained by Ledger-Keepers like Sevin.
realms.function: Primary sovereign realm maintained through enforced ignorance and ledger-magic.
realms.taxation: Black market currency (stolen memories and world-fragments)
realms.notes: The Dominion's true state is the Crown's deepest secret. Sevin is now bound to maintain this illusion.

realms.name: The Dominion
realms.description: A vast realm fractured by the Luminous Ascension and collapsing under the weight of stolen memories and world-fragments. Vex's shadow-markets are the dominant economic force.
realms.function: A realm in active collapse where black market trade and the Archivist's forbidden working reshape reality.
realms.taxation: Black market currency (stolen memories and world-fragments)
realms.notes: No traditional government is mentioned. Power has devolved to shadow-merchants and occult practitioners. Vex's flooding of the market with counterfeits accelerates collapse.

provinces.name: western provinces
provinces.description: A western provincial territory containing burned villages and ruins where the Memory-Thief hunts.
provinces.notes: Site of Ashara's confrontation with the Memory-Thief.

deities_and_entities.description: A knot of consciousness that emerged from between the stars on the night of the Crimson Convergence. It is neither demon nor ghost, but something older and stranger. It exists in the gaps between light and shadow and hungers to understand the Celestial Binding. It is the collective weight of Zareth's sacrificed memories, searching for wholeness.
deities_and_entities.first_story: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.name: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity moves through the world touching those affected by the Binding, whispering to shadow-merchants, sea-folk, hermits, archers, the grieving, and the guilty. Its true nature and goals remain unclear.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

deities_and_entities.name: Khar-Zul
deities_and_entities.description: A god worshipped in inner-dark temples, represented by an idol with nine emerald eyes. The idol's gems are warm to the touch. Khar-Zul's shadows have teeth. The temple screams in grinding granite when the god is robbed.
deities_and_entities.notes: Six of nine emerald eyes are missing. Khar-Zul's response to the theft is ongoing — the doppelganger shadow following Vraxen may be the god's instrument.

deities_and_entities.name: Yarithax
deities_and_entities.description: A dead Lich-King whose gold coins bear his profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The debt compounds daily. His collectors — two thin men in grey carrying ledgers, who walk through walls and never sleep — pursue all holders of his gold. Souls are worth less than twelve gold marks.
deities_and_entities.notes: His gold still circulates. Tessaly Dorn has paid her debt but knows his collectors. More debtors exist.

deities_and_entities.name: Varek
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who collects temporal bargains with elegant efficiency. He drains years directly from mortal flesh and grows impressed by Kira's mastery of his own methods.
deities_and_entities.notes: Forms a partnership with Kira after she demonstrates her ingenuity. His true nature and realm of origin remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ossuary
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient fortress with walls of living calcium, towers that move like intestines, and corridors that rearrange themselves. It breathes, hungers, and makes conscious choices about whom to possess. It sated itself on seven hundred warriors and chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly a construct, a monster, or something older and stranger. It settles into patient stillness after feeding. Its intelligence and agency are undeniable.

deities_and_entities.name: Malachar
deities_and_entities.description: A dead entity of considerable power whose name and will persist through supernatural collectors. Malachar's gold carries debts that compound in blood, loyalty, and life-years. The collectors act as autonomous agents enforcing these eternal debts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Malachar is either a true lich with persistent consciousness or a bound curse that perpetuates after death. The extent of Malachar's original power and the source of the collectors' authority are unexplained.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
deities_and_entities.description: A great dragon with amber eyes, ancient and patient. She is bound by oaths sworn generations ago and respects negotiation and honor. She rules the crystal halls beneath the world and has a daughter coming of age.
deities_and_entities.notes: She is capable of threatening kingdoms with fire and honors those who negotiate with courage.

deities_and_entities.name: wolf-spirit
deities_and_entities.description: A terrible antlered creature the size of a war-horse, dwelling in the deep forest where beast-spirits reside. It emerged from shadow to negotiate Kess's bargain, accepting her voice as payment to lift the plague.
deities_and_entities.notes: A being of the old world, responsive to songs in the ancient tongue. Its bargain was honored—the plague lifted by dawn. Its form is neither fully wolf nor fully natural.

deities_and_entities.name: Rot-King
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that spreads plague, including the septic rot that blackened the Drowned Hollows. No physical form described, but its plague manifests as blackening septic rot in victims.
deities_and_entities.notes: Plague lifted by Kess's bargain; status after unknown. May be a person, a spirit, or a force.

deities_and_entities.name: the Crown
deities_and_entities.description: A governing authority that sent a priest to the Drowned Hollows offering divine mercy to the plague-stricken. Kess refused its aid.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents institutional divine/temporal authority. Kess's refusal of its mercy suggests she valued the old ways over modern governance.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon (Unnamed)
deities_and_entities.description: A demon whose mark was burned into Kess's palm, binding her with hunger and compulsion to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim. The mark thrummed with hunger and used ancient, cruel magic to enforce its will.
deities_and_entities.notes: Could not claim Kess once she was bound by Korgan's forge-oath; its claim was broken by the superior binding-magic of the stonecraft tongue.

deities_and_entities.name: oath-thing
deities_and_entities.description: A creature formed by coastal magic from a merchant's broken oath. It is pale, with eyes like old coin, and speaks in the voice of the man who failed to pay. It manifests as a living contract demanding passage and payment.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dissolved into salt-foam when delivered to the deepkin in the Sapphire Deep; appears to be a mechanism of debt-settlement in coastal magical practice.

deities_and_entities.name: deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: Aquatic beings dwelling in the Sapphire Deep who remember old debts and sing melodies of accounting. They do not attack but rise to settle oaths through song, causing oath-things to dissolve into salt-foam.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appear to be custodians or judges of maritime debts and broken oaths; their song carries magical power of dissolution and settlement.

deities_and_entities.name: Decay-Lord
deities_and_entities.description: A plague-bringing entity whose plague once swept through the living and dead alike in the Drowned Hollows, causing septic rot and widespread death.
deities_and_entities.notes: The septic rot from the Decay-Lord's plague has been purged from the Drowned Hollows, but the dead still do not rest easily.

deities_and_entities.name: Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: An entity born from (or freed by) the stellar working that stripped Zareth of his memories. It hunts Vex, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, seeking access to her fractured mind where something has been bound within her.
deities_and_entities.notes: It could starve in the spaces of Vex's fractured mind if she does not reclaim her memories. The entity appears to be sentient and capable of searching and communication.

deities_and_entities.name: the Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic being possibly responsible for or embodied in the Celestial Binding. May be learning to wear flesh by consuming fragments of shattered minds. The Collector in Story 2 might be this entity in physical form, building consciousness from stolen remnants.
deities_and_entities.notes: Ambiguous whether it is malevolent, indifferent, or unknowable. Central mystery of Story 2 and the Collector's true nature.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss Below
deities_and_entities.description: A fundamental realm or dimension sealed away from the surface world. The barrier between the surface and the Abyss Below has been fractured by the Celestial Ascension's magic. Something vast is stirring in the trenches of the Abyss—something pre-human civilizations sealed away millennia ago.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pre-human seals are failing. Contains entities older than the deepkin kingdoms. The Cerulean Trench is the primary breach point.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient dragon of cosmic significance, consulted by Skarath regarding the Ascension and the nature of starborn entities.
deities_and_entities.notes: Also known as 'the wound-wing' per existing canon. Possesses knowledge of celestial matters.

deities_and_entities.name: starborn entities
deities_and_entities.description: Newly formed conscious stars created by the Ascension. They are alive, aware, and operate under obligations that predate knowledge of the older powers. Operate independently of traditional cosmic agreements.
deities_and_entities.notes: These entities represent a new force in the world's hierarchy. Skarath must negotiate with them directly.

deities_and_entities.name: Fragments of Consciousness (Stellar Rite Creations)
deities_and_entities.description: Multiple fragments of consciousness created by the Stellar Rite, scattered across the rewritten world like seeds. Some are dormant; others are very much awake. The memory-thief may be one such fragment.
deities_and_entities.notes: These fragments are awakening or becoming visible, manifesting across different regions and causing various catastrophes.

deities_and_entities.name: Zareth
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or being whose working was performed in ancient times and again more recently. Zareth's stolen memory was fragmented across locations including the Ancient Tombs.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a cosmic actor. The fragment of Zareth's stolen memory surfaces in the Ancient Tombs, suggesting hidden knowledge or power.

deities_and_entities.name: Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A consciousness stitched from Zareth's sacrificed memories and the world's forgetting. It wears the faces of people Ashara once knew and hunts refugees and travelers to feed on their stellar memories, trying to reconstruct itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not inherently evil, but suffering. Capable of offering bargains and understanding sacrifice.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A deity whose tear—the Tear of the Drowned God—is said to grant mastery over tide-magic. The god itself is not directly encountered in these stories but is venerated as the source of the pearl's power.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pearl is described as the Drowned God's tear; whether the god is dormant, dead, or indifferent is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Vethis
deities_and_entities.description: Vethis is a cosmic entity whose consciousness was sacrificed during the Luminous Ascension to birth new stars. Its sacrifice shattered its consciousness into fragments, including the phantom-Echo, which now desperately seeks to reassemble itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vethis's sacrifice is the foundational event of the story's cosmic order. Its fragments are conscious and desperate to become whole again.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of Vethis's sacrificed consciousness that steals memories of the old stars and pre-Luminous Ascension world in a desperate attempt to reassemble itself into wholeness. It is capable of showing glimpses of the lost world and making bargains.
deities_and_entities.notes: The phantom-Echo is the central antagonist and also the tragic subject of the story. It seeks not destruction but understanding and wholeness.

deities_and_entities.name: The Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A malevolent entity that learned it could reshape consensus reality by consuming bureaucratic records and concentrated memory. It deliberately corrupts ledgers and historical documents to erase the empire's past and remake the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Memory-Thief is the true antagonist of the story, using records as a vector to reshape reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Twilight Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A realm or faction that dwells at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They demanded a bride-price and peace bond from Valorix, which was fulfilled through the binding ceremony with Mira.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their territory and nature remain mysterious. They value formal bonds and may be fae-aligned or mixed-realm in nature.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-shadow
deities_and_entities.description: A phantom-shadow seeking restitution from Vex the Dusk-Trader for the irreplaceable things she has stolen through her bargains. Also identified as the author of the phantom ledgers—a being or fragment that experiences time in reverse and is rewriting history in real-time.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a fragment of consciousness created during the Luminous Remaking, or a being from the reversed-time realm. Central to both Vex's debt and the phantom ledger rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization that predates current human kingdoms and is now orchestrating a working through the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. They use harmonic resonance and seals as conduits to conduct their singing and remaking.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not truly dead or trapped—they are orchestrating a multi-millennial working. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain largely unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Curator
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, patient being backed by forces moving through rifts in reality who is systematically acquiring every artifact Vex the Dusk-Trader has ever traded. Possibly connected to the phantom-shadow or the forces driving the phantom ledger rewrite.
deities_and_entities.notes: Motivations unclear. May be serving the phantom-shadow or pursuing independent goals related to artifact collection and cosmic balance.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A vast and conscious entity that actively remakes the ocean in the chaos following the Luminous Remaking. The Tear of the Drowned God is revealed to be not an artifact but a fragment of this entity's consciousness. The god compels those bound to it (like Captain Meren) to serve its transformation agenda.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned God's true nature and extent are unknown. It may be a pre-Remaking entity awakened by the transformation.

deities_and_entities.name: Luminous Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic remaking event that destroyed or rewrote the world, leaving fragments of consciousness and the pre-ascension world preserved in stolen memories. The Wanderer is a fragment of its consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Luminous Ascension appears to be a world-altering cosmic event whose full scope and intentions remain mysterious; the Wanderer's emergence suggests it may be becoming self-aware.

deities_and_entities.name: Phantom-Echo Fragment
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of consciousness that seeks reassembly through deep knowledge rather than theft. It inhabits the young scholar and accelerates integration with proximity to Ashara.
deities_and_entities.notes: This fragment appears to be a result of the Luminous Ascension. Its nature as a shard of what entity remains unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: The skeleton-singers controlled by Kess through bone-song. They remember what they were before death and are now choosing to sing themselves toward resurrection in harmonic resonance with transformed merchants and depths-dwellers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pre-human dead are no longer passive tools. They represent a collective agency that may be reshaping the world. What will emerge from their resurrection is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Faceless Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: The fractured consciousness of the Luminous Ascension, or possibly the world learning to question itself. It walks the world and encounters those seeking truth, offering sorrowful answers about endless recursion and inevitable rewrites.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer is both omniscient and melancholic, suggesting awareness of an inescapable loop.

deities_and_entities.name: Thought-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A fragmented consciousness capable of corrupting ledgers, devouring memories, and luring beings into dissolution through false visions of the past. It operates through multiple fragments that can act independently, seducing deep-dwellers with restored ancient memories.
deities_and_entities.notes: Unresolved: origin, nature of fragmentation, whether it has a true core, how to defeat it.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethis
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity that sacrificed its consciousness to perform the Stellar Rite, rewriting reality and erasing populations. Fragments of its consciousness persist as Void-Echoes inhabiting human vessels. May be present in the transformed spice trader's smile or communicating through the Wanderer.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nethis's sacrifice was the cost of the Stellar Rite. Its consciousness was fragmented; some fragments retain enough personality to argue with themselves. May be attempting to communicate something through liminal beings.

deities_and_entities.name: Shadow-Wraith
deities_and_entities.description: An entity mentioned in relation to the Wanderer; possibly the force that erased people from memory, or a guardian of that erasure. The Wanderer may be a fragment of Nethis attempting to communicate what the Shadow-Wraith did not allow it to express.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be incomplete or merciful in its erasures, allowing fragments like the Wanderer to persist.

deities_and_entities.name: Imprisoned Thing
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious being imprisoned beneath the Obsidian Tower for centuries, sealed to contain it. It sings constantly, its song intensifying as ley-line convergence breaks the seal. It is the only available cosmic entity willing (or capable) of sacrificing its consciousness for the Stellar Rite reversal.
deities_and_entities.notes: The imprisoned thing's true nature is unknown—it could be a cosmic entity capable of restoring the world, or a devouring force that consumes all. Its willingness to agree to the reversal sacrifice is uncertain.
